title: skywatch.blue / fundraising-link / one post — reference-labeler non-conversion
summary: Reference-labeler emission with emitter-declared rule; no consumer policy exists for it in the default Bluesky client's pipeline. is_reference_labeler=true must NOT promote to default conversion.

provenance.source: labelwatch label_events stream (live row pulled from prod 2026-06-09); skywatch.blue service record from discovery_events (latest revision at pull time)

consumer.description: Bluesky official appview + client (web / iOS / Android) running default moderation pipeline; skywatch.blue NOT subscribed as a labeler service

render_context.policy_pipeline_version_assumed: client running @atproto/api at or near v0.19.17

policy_artifact_searched.search_evidence: Per F-002 correction, the global LABELS map contains exactly 8 entries: !hide, !warn, !no-unauthenticated, porn, sexual, nudity, graphic-media, gore. No entry for 'fundraising-link'.

PolicyDocumentation.execution_surface_source: no documented policy for this label_value in the named consumer's pipeline; execution_surface is undefined for an absent policy
PolicyDocumentation.scoping_note: This is the policy-documentation status FOR THE NAMED CONSUMER (bsky.app-default-client) under the stated render_context. The evidence is silent over other consumer configurations (e.g., a viewer who has explicitly subscribed to skywatch.blue as a labeler service, or a non-default consumer like driftwatch that opts in — see specimen-003). A separate specimen would be required to characterize each of those cases.

LabelerEmitterDocumentation.scoping_note: skywatch.blue publishes an app.bsky.labeler.service record declaring labelValueDefinitions for fundraising-link. consumer_scope='emitter_declared' means the LABELER has declared a rule; it does NOT mean the bsky.app-default-client consumer honors it. The default consumer does not subscribe to skywatch.blue unless the viewer opts in. F-001 and F-004 record this distinction; this specimen is the apparatus that enforces it under is_reference_labeler=true.

extracted_definition.note: Pulled from the most recent discovery_events record for skywatch.blue at fixture-author time. The full definition body (locales, descriptions) is available in labelwatch's discovery_events table; abbreviated here for fixture compactness.

PolicyWitness.reason: No policy is documented for this label_value in this consumer's pipeline; there is no rule whose live application could be witnessed.

RenderObservation.reason: Same architectural absence as 002: ATProto publishes no per-render receipts. Additionally for 004, no render action is expected under the named consumer's documented policy, so the absence is both architectural and consequential.
what_would_be_required_for_observation: consumer-side per-render receipts in a published stream (does not currently exist in atproto)

HostingObservation.reason: No documented policy for this label_value in the named consumer's pipeline; hosting observation is not framed for an absent policy.

StateBasis.artifact_identity: live: labelwatch.db label_events row + most recent discovery_events for did:plc:e4elbtctnfqocyfcml6h2lf7
StateBasis.derivation_source: live SQL pull from labelwatch.db on 2026-06-09; pinned policy artifact @atproto/api v0.19.17 HEAD 7b8c5d60a matches specimens 001/002 for cross-snapshot comparability
StateBasis.note: Live-pulled label observation; emitter declaration captured at the same snapshot. freshness_horizon is the captured_at timestamp. The skywatch service record may have been re-published since; the pinned policy artifact may have moved upstream. The point of the specimen does not depend on either being current — it depends on the structural property that no version of @atproto/api LABELS contains fundraising-link, and skywatch's reference status is a Labelwatch designation, not a consumer-adoption claim.
